data.description: ASIAN DEFENCE STOCKS ARE DRAWING FRESH INVESTOR ATTENTION AMID A GLOBAL ARMS EXPANSION TRIGGERED BY THE IRAN CONFLICT, WITH SOME INVESTORS VIEWING THE SECTOR AS A LONG-TERM GROWTH OPPORTUNITY RATHER THAN A SHORT-TERM GEOPOLITICAL BET.
JAPAN EARLY TRADE: NIKKEI FUTURES UP 0.4%; 10-YEAR JGB FUTURES GAIN 0.07 POINT.
OIL CLIMBED, THE DOLLAR STRENGTHENED AND U.S. EQUITY FUTURES DIPPED AS RISK APPETITE WEAKENED FOLLOWING A FRESH ESCALATION IN U.S.-IRAN TENSIONS OVER THE WEEKEND.

data.title: Faraday Future Announces $45 Million in New Financing with a U.S. Institutional Investor to Strongly Support the Execution of Its EAI Robotics & EAI EV Business; Plans to Pursue Long-Term Strategic Cooperation with the Investor for Mutual Benefit
data.description: Faraday Future Intelligent Electric Inc., a California-based global company, disclosed a recent $45 million financing agreement with a U.S. institutional investor. The full amount was received promptly on the same day. This funding will greatly assist in advancing the company's Embodied AI ecosystem strategy.

data.title: SK hynix Begins Mass Production of 192GB SOCAMM2  'Setting a New Standard for AI Server Memory Performance'
data.description: A company has commenced mass production of high capacity products totaling 192GB specifically for the NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform. These products aim to enhance power efficiency through the utilization of high density DRAM built on the newest 1cnm process. The company plans to work closely with NVIDIA to address bottlenecks in AI infrastructure and deliver optimized solutions.

data.title: Sutro Biopharma Presents Promising Preclinical Data Across its Pipeline of Next-Generation Single and Dual-Payload ADC Programs at AACR 2026
data.description: The drug STRO-004 has shown strong and consistent anti-cancer effects in various models of solid tumors expressing the targeted protein (TF), outperforming other similar antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs) in terms of efficacy.
NEW ZEALAND PM CHRISTOPHER LUXON SAYS HE IS NOT CONSIDERING RESIGNING AHEAD OF NOVEMBER'S ELECTION DESPITE POLLS SHOWING DECLINING SUPPORT FOR HIM AND HIS NATIONAL PARTY.
NAB: CET1 RATIO IMPACTED BY ~20BPS FROM NZD WEAKNESS AND RATE VOLATILITY, WITH PRO FORMA CET1 RATIO AT MARCH 2026 TARGETED ABOVE 12%.
NAB: THE BANK PLANS A 1.5% DRP DISCOUNT WITH PARTIAL UNDERWRITING, AIMING TO RAISE UP TO A$1.8B AND ADD APPROXIMATELY 40BPS TO CET1 IN 2H26.
NAB ANTICIPATES A$706M IN CREDIT IMPAIRMENT CHARGES FOR 1H26, REVIEWING PROVISIONING AND CAPITAL SETTINGS TO REFLECT ELEVATED RISK WITH COLLECTIVE PROVISIONS TO CREDIT RWA AT 1.35%.
SK HYNIX: COMMENCES MANUFACTURING OF SOCAMN2 CHIPS ENGINEERED FOR NVIDIA'S VERA RUBIN PROCESSORS.
U.S. CRUDE OIL SURGES OVER 7.5%, TRADING ABOVE $90.
EUROPEAN GAS SURGES AS MUCH AS 9.8% AS IRAN SHUTS THE STRAIT OF HORMUZ ONCE MORE.
IRAN'S MILITARY: CARRIED OUT DRONE STRIKES ON SEVERAL U.S. NAVAL VESSELS FOLLOWING THE INCIDENT.
IRAN: THE VESSEL TARGETED BY THE U.S. WAS EN ROUTE FROM CHINA TO IRAN.
JAPAN PM TAKAICHI'S APPROVAL DROPS TO 53% PER MAINICHI; YOMIURI POLL SHOWS RATING AT 66%, DOWN 5 POINTS.
DOLLAR/YEN RISES 0.22% TO 159. STERLING FALLS 0.24% TO $1.3486. EURO DROPS 0.2% TO $1.174.
BRITISH FOREIGN OFFICE: NORTH KOREA'S BALLISTIC MISSILE TESTS ON 19 APRIL CONSTITUTE ANOTHER VIOLATION OF UN SECURITY COUNCIL RESOLUTIONS.

NEW ZEALAND TRADE BALANCE (MAR): SURPLUS OF NZ$698M VS REVISED DEFICIT OF NZ$365M PRIOR. EXPORTS ROSE TO NZ$7.94B FROM NZ$6.52B. IMPORTS AT NZ$7.25B FROM NZ$6.88B. 12-MONTH TRAILING DEFICIT WIDENED TO NZ$3.19B.
IRAN'S MILITARY ACCUSES THE U.S. OF FIRING ON AN IRANIAN COMMERCIAL VESSEL IN VIOLATION OF THE CEASEFIRE, WARNING OF RETALIATION OVER WHAT IT TERMS "MARITIME PIRACY."
